data.title: Main Street expects record net asset value per share for Q1
data.description:
Main Street Capital (NYSE:MAIN) expects a new record for net asset value per share for the eleventh consecutive quarter for Q1.

The company’s preliminary estimate of net asset value as of March 31, 2025 is $32.00 to $32.06, representing an increase of 1.1% to 1.3% from $31.65 as of December 31, 2024. The estimated growth is primarily due to the net fair value increase on the investment portfolio resulting from the net unrealized appreciation and the net realized loss on portfolio investments.

Q1 net investment income is estimated in the range of $1.00 to $1.02 per share, while distributable net investment income is projected between $1.06 and $1.08 a share. The company estimates that it generated a quarterly annualized return on equity of over 16% for in the quarter.

Main Street (NYSE:MAIN) will release its first quarter 2025 results on May 8, 2025, after the financial markets close.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Skeena files Eskay Creek environmental assessment application
data.description:
Skeena Resources (NYSE:SKE) +2.7% pre-market Wednesday after saying it filed its environmental assessment application for the planned Eskay Creek gold-silver project with British Columbia's Environmental Assessment Office for joint review with the Tahltan Nation, and the company said it expects to receive the environmental assessment certificate for the project in Q4.

Skeena (NYSE:SKE) said the project's economic impact study reveals significant benefits, including an estimated $14B impact on gross domestic product over the life of the mine; an expected $3.6B in direct, indirect and induced tax revenue to B.C. and Canada over the life of the mine; and significant production of critical minerals including antimony, zinc, lead and copper, in addition to gold and silver.

"Eskay Creek will be a key contributor to regional economic growth and community development for nearly two decades... [and] is poised to become Canada's largest silver mine, producing a vital metal that will support a cleaner future," Skeena (SKE) CEO Randy Reichert said.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: U.S. Bancorp tops Q1 estimates, FY revenue outlook lags
Movado says it will raise some prices to offset tariff costs
data.description:
Movado Group (NYSE:MOV) reported revenue was up 1.1% year-over-year to $181.5 million in Q4. Gross margin improved to 54.2% of sales during the quarter that ended on January 31 vs. 53.5% a year ago. Adjusted EPS was reported at $0.51 vs. $0.39 a year ago.

CEO Efraim Grinberg said that despite a challenging macroeconomic backdrop, the company delivered net sales growth in the quarter and also expanded gross profit margin while increasing marketing spending to support future growth. "Given the ongoing uncertainty within the global retail environment, tariffs, and economic unrest that may ensue, we will continue to focus on the areas that we can control," he noted. Looking ahead, Movado (NYSE:MOV) plans to increase its focus on reducing go-forward operating expenses.

Movado (MOV) pulled its fiscal 2026 outlook due to the current economic uncertainty and the unpredictable impact of recent tariff developments on its business. Movado (MOV) said it is planning to take actions to partially mitigate the impact of the recent tariff changes, including select price increases at the wholesale and retail levels.

Shares of Movado (MOV) were up 20.1% in premarket action on very light volume.

data.title: European markets dip as investors remain cautious with tariff turbulence, ASML falls
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects market movements as of 7.00am ET.

London (UKX) -0.36% to 8,219. UK's inflation rate drops to 2.6%, below estimates. The British Pound rose for a seventh straight day to $1.327, heading for its longest winning streak since July.

Germany (DAX:IND) -0.55% to 21,137. 

France (CAC:IND) -0.55% to 7,295. 

The annual inflation rate in Austria slowed to 2.9% in March.

Producer prices in the Czech Republic decreased by 0.3% Y/Y in March.

Euro Area inflation falls to 2.2% in March, meeting estimates.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged 0.79% lower to 504.04, with tech stocks taking the hardest hit. Investor sentiment globally remained cautious as Trump’s rapidly evolving trade policies kept traders alarmed.

Investors digested a fresh round of corporate earnings. ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) dropped 5% after Q1 revenue miss. Heineken (OTCQX:HEINY) revenue beats estimates, but beer sales wane.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points to 4.34%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 2.51%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 3 basis points to 4.62%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: The Travelers Companies Non-GAAP EPS of $1.91, net written premiums of $10.52B misses by $450M
data.description:
• The Travelers Companies press release (NYSE:TRV): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.91.
  • Net written premiums of $10.52B (+3.8% Y/Y) misses by $450M.
  • Board of Directors Declares 5% Increase in Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend to $1.10 per Share
  • Consolidated combined ratio of 102.5%; and underlying combined ratio of 84.8%, a 2.9 point improvement.
  • Catastrophe losses of $2.266 billion pre-tax, primarily driven by the January 2025 California wildfires.

data.title: Ferguson Enterprises closes two acquisitions
data.description:
• Ferguson Enterprises (NYSE:FERG) said it has closed the acquisitions of Independent Pipe & Supply and National Fire Equipment and National Fire Fabrication.
  • Independent Pipe & Supply is a distributor of pipe, valves, and other products and has eight locations. 
  • The deal boosts Ferguson’s existing footprint and fabrication capabilities in the New England area.
  • National Fire deals with fire and fabrication products and services and has seven locations across eastern and western Canada.
  • The deal enables the expansion of Ferguson's Fire & Fabrication customer group strategy in Canada and also enhances National Fire’s product offering to its customers. 
  • National Fire’s business operations will be integrated into Ferguson’s unit in Canada, Wolseley Canada.
  • The Independent Pipe & Supply deal closed on March 31, and the National Fire deal closed on April 14.
